item.description:
ChainLink 0.3.507

Half the disk, clearer form numbers, and a stack of fixes.

Ride file archive: half the size

  • Original ride files are now stored compressed — on first launch this build quietly converts your existing archive, roughly halving the space it takes. Nothing changes in how the app behaves or performs.
  • Settings > Data now shows the archive's size and file count, so you can see what's on this device.

Form (TSB) finally speaks plainly

  • The Training and Fitness pages' metric pills carry a one-word verdict — Fresh / Neutral / Building / Overreached / Too Fresh — with honest colours: green only for genuine race freshness, amber for the productive fatigue of a training block, red only for real overreach. No more "red = you trained properly".
  • The charts' form shading follows the same bands, day by day — a hard block reads amber, and only the depth below −30 turns red.
  • The "What this means" readout now uses the same rounded numbers as the pills and names both terms ("Fitness (CTL) 28…").

Training plans: honest week accounting

  • Plans created mid-week no longer mis-count: the current week, the plan page's week picker and the compliance verdict all follow the plan's own Monday-anchored weeks. A big week no longer reads "behind plan" because most of it happened before you pressed Generate.

Fixed

  • Coach Insights cards expand on tap to show their full text (they were clamped to two lines).
  • Clicking an upcoming event on the Overview goes to the Events page — the editor only opens from the right-click "Edit in Events" action.
  • The Fitness page's three sub-page cards (Power Profile, Courses, Performance Management) work again on the Mac.

ChainLink 0.3.499

Coach Insights numbers now match the calendar.

Fixed

  • Coach Insights no longer double-counts rides that arrive from two connected services (e.g. Garmin and Strava). Training-load warnings, the Monday weekly review and record detection now use the same numbers as the training calendar — no more "1088 TSS" cards for a 558-TSS week.
  • Load warnings are now honest about their window: they look at your last 7 days (rolling), not the calendar week.

ChainLink 0.3.498

CORE body temperature, smarter training plans, and a stack of fixes.

New: CORE body temperature (greenteg CORE sensor)

  • Ride with a CORE sensor paired to your head unit and ChainLink reads the data straight from your ride files — nothing to set up. You get a Core Temp graph on the ride page and a plain-English heat dose ("32 min at or above 38.5 °C — a solid heat dose").
  • The AI coach sees your measured heat exposure alongside HRV, and travel advice for hot events is graded against your actual heat sessions from the last three weeks instead of a guess.
  • It's not just new rides: on first launch this build quietly re-reads your existing ride files, so past rides recorded with a CORE sensor get their temperature graphs and heat doses too — no re-import needed.

Training plans got a bigger playbook

  • Generated plans now vary the structure of workouts, not just the length: over-unders, ladders, 40/20 microbursts, hard-start reps, float sets, cadence drills and more. No shape repeats until you've seen them all, and the session card always describes exactly what the exported workout will make you ride.
  • One active plan at a time: starting an event plan now ends a running maintenance block (with a note) instead of double-counting your rides.
  • Maintenance plans gained the full action menu — adjust the block, share it as a PDF, end it.

Garmin Connect: history import hardened

  • The full-history import now paces itself around Garmin's rate limits, takes a breather and carries on when they bite, and re-checks the whole account once so previously missed rides are picked up automatically.
  • A second device no longer re-downloads everything — it recognises rides that already arrived via iCloud.

Small things

  • The AI Coach recovers automatically when Google retires the Gemini model behind your key (the "model no longer supported" error).
  • Settings > Invite a tester: QR codes and a ready-made invite card for both the Mac and iPhone/iPad versions.
  • Milestones on the Goals page: switch between Lifetime and This Year, the ladder re-centres on where you are, and passed milestones light up.
  • Plan sheets no longer clip their buttons on a short window, and sharing a plan PDF works properly.
